definition: The moment of knowing associated with a sense field and its object.
notes: Viññāṇa refers to the consciousness that arises dependent on a sense field and an object. The project keeps consciousness as the default because it is the clearest stable rendering in both aggregate and dependent-origination contexts. Awareness remains acceptable in explanatory prose, but only where it does not imply a permanent substrate or diffuse background self.

context_rules.context: When referring to the six sense consciousnesses
When explanatory prose needs a smoother English line
context_rules.notes: Use carefully and do not imply a permanent substrate.

example_phrases.notes: Illustrates one of the six consciousnesses.
A debated phrase; included here as an example only.
